topic: Sara Duterte impeachment trial — defense procedural tactics vs. testimonial clarity
tweet1: Atty. Vinluan objected when prosecutors briefly spoke Waray during the Duterte impeachment trial, demanding 'Filipino or English only' — then spent the day arguing over receipt dates instead of the assassination threat itself.
tweet2: Defense counsel argued Rule 130 Sec 16 violations over language use. Prosecution witness Atty. Lotoc confirmed: 'The Vice President committed a crime.' Defense response? Debate subpoena timestamps from November 6 vs. November 23.
tweet3: Sen. Bam Aquino noted the defense never specifically denied that Sara Duterte contracted someone to kill Marcos. Vinluan claimed it was 'already denied' — but pointed to a 'blanket denial' in written pleadings, not testimony.
tweet4: Procedural theater works when the public forgets what's actually on trial. Here's the test: by Day 5, will anyone remember the threat itself — or just the arguments about paperwork? Watch which one the defense keeps amplifying.
